patches.hypothesis: The constructor does not validate if the column dimension exceeds a reasonable limit, which causes the test expecting a NumberIsTooLargeException to fail.
patches.changes: + added validation for columnDimension to throw NumberIsTooLargeException if it exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE - 1
patches.diff: + added validation for columnDimension to throw NumberIsTooLargeException if it exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE - 1

patches.hypothesis: The constructor does not validate the input dimensions, specifically checking if the column dimension exceeds the maximum allowed value. We need to add a check to throw NumberIsTooLargeException if the column dimension is too large.
patches.changes: + if (columnDimension > Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8) { throw new NumberIsTooLargeException(columnDimension, Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8, true); }
patches.diff: + if (columnDimension > Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8) { throw new NumberIsTooLargeException(columnDimension, Integer.MAX_VALUE - 8, true); }

patches.hypothesis: The refined fix will add checks to ensure that rowDimension and columnDimension are non-negative and that their product does not exceed Integer.MAX_VALUE, thus handling general cases and avoiding overfitting.
patches.original_hypothesis: The constructor does not validate if the column dimension is too large, which leads to the test expecting a NumberIsTooLargeException to be thrown not occurring.
